results.title: Uzbekistan advances global labor standards at union conference
results.description: Trade union leaders from Uzbekistan, representatives of international labor organizations, and industry groups gathered in Tashkent to discuss the implementation of international standards in trade union organization, with a focus on strengthening workers’ rights and developing social dialogue, AzerNEWS reports, citing Trend.

results.title: UAE should be held accountable for supporting US strikes: Iran
results.description: Iran says the UAE supported recent US military strikes against Tehran in exchange for trade concessions from Washington, citing a newly released US document.

results.title: Ann Widdecombe investigation update as police issue new statement
results.description: The former Conservative Minister and MP for Maidstone and The Weald was found dead at her home on Dartmoor on Thursday morning having sustained serious head injuries

results.description: Staff Reporter: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman said on Saturday that the government is working to enhance the capacity of district and upazila-level hospitals to improve access to healthcare services across the country. results.title: American Aid Worker Tests Positive for Ebola in Congo Outbreak
results.description: A U.S. citizen working with a humanitarian organization in Congo has tested positive for Ebola as the country battles what health officials call the fastest-growing outbreak in African history, with 1,830 cases and 648 deaths so far.

results.title: Goshen Backlot Boils As 'The Chosen' Studio Clashes With Crowdfunders
results.description: Crowdfunders allege The Chosen's studio shortchanged backers; filings show arbitration history and ongoing disputes as Season 7 filmed in Goshen.

results.title: Coron serves more than stunning views
results.description: Nearly a decade after my first visit to Coron, one thing remained unchanged: the astonishing cla­rity of its waters. There was a storm during my first visit and the other members of our group who had been booked on a later flight were unable to land in Busuanga. Instead, their plane shuddered and shook as it made its way back to Manila while we remained blissfully unaware. As guests of Sunlight Ecotourism Island Resort (Setir) a few weeks ago, some days were cloudy but the weather cooperated and select lifestyle writers had the opportunity to experience their two properties, the city hotel and the island resort. The former is a good jump-off point for tourists who plan to go island-hopping around Coron. The hotel is equipped to arrange tours that can include two or three islands as well as a picnic lunch served boodle-style on Banol Island. Our first stop was Kayangan Lake where we hiked up uneven steps made of poured concrete. Make sure to pack sturdy, water- and slip-resistant footwear. The view from the top is one of Coron’s most photographed so get in line to have some photos taken before clambering more concrete steps down to the lake. That morning, there were few tourists so we had the place to ourselves. The wearing of life vests while in the lake is compulsory. One of our companions, a swimmer, removed his and was reprimanded. We also had stops at Barracuda Lake and the Twin Lagoons with their bottle-green waters but what really mesmerized and frightened me simultaneously was Smith Coral Garden. Our speedboat just stopped in the middle of the ocean and one by one we jumped into the crystal-clear water. As I bobbed on the water’s surface, gazing through my mask at the dizzying number of fish and corals (fan, brain, staghorn), it felt like I was flying. I could have stayed there all afternoon but when I looked up at one point, the boat was several yards away from me; I hadn’t noticed the current pulling me away. It was a struggle swimming back to the boat and I gladly grasped the lifesaver that the staff tossed to me and which they they used to haul me back. By the time we reached Banol for the picnic lunch, we were ravenous and made short work of the feast that included fried chicken, steamed shrimps, crab and mussels arranged on a long bed of rice. We washed everything down with fresh coconut water. Island hopping done, we headed to Setir (sunlighthotelsandresorts.com) which is 30 to 45 minutes away from Coron by speedboat. The resort is composed of private villas set over the water with newer rooms outfitted with glass floors, jacuzzi or direct access to the water. During the rainy season, when island hopping isn’t feasible, the resort offers a dining “passport” to guests so they can try out the different outlets that specialize in a range of cuisines from Filipino favorites and Korean barbecue to bistro-style fare and Japanese teppanyaki. At the Japanese outlet Hikari one evening, we were entertained by the Filipino chef who dazzled us with his acrobatic-like skills on the grill using only his trusty spatula. He prepared several dishes including yasai itame (sautéed vegetables), salmon, steak and fried rice. Breakfasts at Setir were at Mangrove, the resort’s open-air dining destination that is bounded on two sides by mangrove trees. The buffet selection may be limited but the two ladies at the omelet station are the real thing. They were quick and efficient, flipping omelets to order one after another. To experience full vacation mode at Setir, book a massage at the spa and ask for Jayfel. She used her elbows and strong forearms to ease out the knots on my shoulders and back. If I had to pinpoint my favorite parts of this trip, they would definitely include the snorkeling at Smith, the calming view of the water from the villa and the garlic-fried rice and steak at Hikari.

results.title: Rubio: US to use every tool to address national security threats posed by Cuba
results.description: The United States will continue to use every tool at its disposal to address the national security threats posed by Cuba, US State Secretary Marco Rubio said, Report informs.

results.title: Coaching Staff Contracts Set to Expire After England Series
results.description: As the Indian cricket team faces a challenging series against England, the contracts of Gautam Gambhir's coaching staff are set to expire. With recent poor performances, including a series loss to Ireland and a disappointing T20I series against England, the BCCI plans to hold a review meeting to discuss the team's shortcomings. The future of key coaching staff members remains uncertain, particularly regarding fielding coach roles. This article explores the implications of these developments and what they mean for the team's future performance.
